curation_notes: The patch adds an assertion in the block() function in chacha20/src/cipher.rs to check that the counter does not exceed MAX_BLOCKS (2^32) before generating a keystream block. There are two implementations of block() gated by cfg attributes (x86/x86_64 vs other architectures), and both received the same fix. Sibling files legacy.rs and xchacha20.rs delegate to the same Cipher type and are not independently vulnerable. The pre-assigned class integer-overflow (CWE-190) is correct — the root cause is a 32-bit counter wrap-around.
